figures.caption: Table 1: Results on the WOD-E2E. ↑ indicates higher is better, while ↓ indicates lower is better. Best results are shown in bold, and second-best results are underlined.
Table 2: Results on the NAVSIM v1. All metrics are reported with one decimal place, and higher is better for all metrics. Best results are shown in bold, and second-best results are underlined.
Figure 1: Comparison of three training paradigms for Driving VLAs.

Figure 5: Qualitative examples of language-action alignment. SFT can predict correct high-level meta-actions but still generate inconsistent trajectories. With the trajectory–meta-action consistency reward, DriveMA better translates predicted meta-actions into executable trajectories.

Figure 3: Turn-level credit assignment RL in DriveMA. DriveMA assigns meta-action rewards to the decision turn and trajectory/consistency rewards to the planning turn. Rewards are normalized within each turn, enabling dense supervision and precise credit assignment for language-action alignment.
